skill.name: tailwind-design-system
skill.description: Build scalable design systems with Tailwind CSS, design tokens, component libraries, and responsive patterns.
skill.summary: Create production-ready design systems using Tailwind CSS with design tokens, component variants, and accessibility patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 78 potential issues, all confirmed as false positives. The 'backtick execution' patterns are Markdown code fences (```) for code samples. URLs are documentation references. localStorage usage is standard theme persistence. No executable code or security risks present.

content.user_title: Build design systems with Tailwind CSS
content.value_statement: Creating consistent design systems is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides proven patterns for building scalable component libraries with Tailwind CSS, design tokens, and accessibility built-in.
actual_capabilities: Generate Tailwind CSS configuration with semantic design tokens
Create component variants using CVA (Class Variance Authority)
Build compound components with proper React patterns
Implement responsive grid systems and layouts
Add dark mode support with CSS variables
Create accessible form components with validation
limitations: Requires React and TypeScript project setup
Does not include visual design or branding guidance
Focused on utility-first CSS approach only
Does not provide design tool integrations (Figma, Sketch)

use_cases.title: Startup Component Library
use_cases.description: Build a complete component library from scratch for a new product, including buttons, cards, forms, and dialogs with consistent styling.

use_cases.title: Design System Migration
use_cases.description: Migrate an existing CSS or styled-components codebase to Tailwind CSS with proper design token structure and component patterns.

use_cases.title: Accessible Form Implementation
use_cases.description: Create accessible form components with proper validation, error states, and keyboard navigation following WCAG guidelines.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building admin dashboards and SaaS applications

prompt_templates.title: Basic Button Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reusable Button component using Tailwind CSS and CVA. Include variants for primary, secondary, outline, and ghost styles. Add size variants (sm, md, lg) and a disabled state.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new component library and need foundational components.

prompt_templates.title: Design Token Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Tailwind CSS configuration file with semantic color tokens. Include primary, secondary, destructive, muted, accent, background, foreground, and border colors. Support both light and dark mode using CSS variables.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up the foundation for a design system.

prompt_templates.title: Compound Card Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Card component using the compound component pattern. Create Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, and CardFooter subcomponents. Use forwardRef for proper React composition.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating flexible layout components for content display.

prompt_templates.title: Dark Mode Theme Provider
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a complete dark mode solution with a ThemeProvider component. Support system preference detection, localStorage persistence, and manual toggle. Include a ThemeToggle button component with sun/moon icons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding theme switching to an existing application.

output_examples.input: Create a button with Tailwind CSS
output: Button component created with CVA variants
Includes: default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link variants
Size options: default, sm, lg, icon
Accessibility features: focus-visible ring, disabled state

output_examples.input: Set up dark mode for my app
output: ThemeProvider component with system preference detection
Tailwind config extended with darkMode: class

best_practices: Use CSS variables for runtime theming and design tokens
Compose variants with CVA for type-safe component APIs
Always include focus-visible states for keyboard accessibility
anti_patterns: Using arbitrary values like h-[500px] instead of extending the theme
Hardcoding colors directly in components instead of semantic tokens
Skipping dark mode styles when building new components

faq.question: What is CVA and why should I use it?
faq.answer: CVA (Class Variance Authority) is a library for creating type-safe component variants. It allows you to define base styles and variant options that compose cleanly, with full TypeScript support.

faq.question: How do I add custom colors to my design system?
faq.answer: Extend the Tailwind theme in tailwind.config.ts using CSS variables. Define the color in both light and dark mode in your globals.css file for full theming support.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with vanilla CSS or other frameworks?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Tailwind CSS specifically. For vanilla CSS or other frameworks like styled-components, different patterns and approaches would be needed.

faq.question: How do I handle component accessibility?
faq.answer: Always include proper ARIA attributes, focus states, and keyboard navigation. Use semantic HTML elements and ensure proper heading hierarchy. Test with screen readers.

faq.question: What is the compound component pattern?
faq.answer: Compound components work together to create a flexible UI. Examples include Select with SelectTrigger, SelectValue, SelectContent or Card with CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent. This pattern enables clean composition.

faq.question: How do I migrate from Bootstrap or Material UI?
faq.answer: Start by setting up design tokens that match your existing design. Then migrate components one at a time, starting with foundational elements like buttons and inputs. Use the implementation playbook for patterns.
